data.description:
## Context
Operation certificates become asymmetric on the foundation substrate (namespace `rebar.opcert.v1`), signed by an environment's Ed25519 key and verifiable by anyone against its public key, binding the ticket/state so they cannot be replayed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Op-certs are produced/verified via the foundation substrate with an Ed25519 environment key (namespace `rebar.opcert.v1`); a test asserts a cert signed by env E verifies against E's public key and fails against a different key.
- [ ] The certificate subject binds `{ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position}`; a test asserts it cannot be replayed onto a different ticket or a mutated state.
data.title: op-cert: asymmetric environment-signed kind + subject binding
